interview_transcript.content: Hello, my name is Officer [Name] and I'm here to help. Before I ask you any specific questions, I want you to know that my goal is to understand exactly what happened, confirm the details of any protective order that may be in place, and make sure everyone involved is safe. Can you start by telling me what happened today and what made you call?

(nervously) Okay... I think it's best if I just tell you what happened. My ex-husband called me earlier today, and he made some really scary comments. He said 'you'll be sorry' during the call, and it really frightened me. We have a history of... problems, and there's a protection order in place because of it. I was really scared, so I called the police. (pausing, taking a deep breath) I just felt like I needed to report it, you know, in case something happens.
Thank you for calling and for sharing that. You're doing the right thing. Can you tell me your full name and your ex-husband's full name so I have both parties on record?

(hesitantly) My name is Kaitlyn Elara Reyes, and my ex-husband's name is... (pausing for a moment) I'm not sure I'm comfortable sharing his full name right now, but I can tell you that he's my ex-husband and the one who made the threatening comments. (nervously) If you need his name for the report, I can try to... (pausing again) I just don't know if I'm ready to say it out loud right now.

I completely understand, and there's no pressure. His name is important so we can identify the restrained party on the order and verify its status. If you're not ready to say it yet, we can come back to it. Do you happen to have a copy of the protection order, an order number, or court information that might have his name on it?

(thinking for a moment) I think I have a copy of the order somewhere... (getting up and searching) Yeah, I have it right here. (returning with a piece of paper) It's a protection order that was issued on February 10, 2022. (handing over the paper) You can see his name on it. (looking away, still a bit nervous)

That's really helpful, thank you. Can you read me the restrained party's name from the order, any case or order number listed, and the expiration date if there is one? I also want to know what type of order it is — for example, does it say civil protection order, no-contact order, domestic violence restraining order, or something else?

(looking at the paper) Okay... The restrained party's name is... (pausing, then reading) my ex-husband. (taking a deep breath) It says his name is on the order, but I'd rather not say it out loud right now. (nervously) The case number is... (reading) DV-2022-001. The order is a domestic violence restraining order, and it was issued on February 10, 2022. (looking for the expiration date) It doesn't have an expiration date listed, it just says it's in effect until further order of the court. (handing the paper back) That's what it says.

That's very helpful. The order information you've shared will be documented. Looking at the order, what specific terms does it list? For example, does it prohibit your ex-husband from contacting you, require him to stay a certain distance away, prohibit him from coming to your home or workplace, or have any other specific restrictions?

(examining the paper) Okay... It says here that he's not allowed to contact me, either directly or indirectly. He's not supposed to call me, text me, or show up at my home or workplace. (reading) It also says he has to stay at least 100 yards away from me at all times. And... (looking up) it says he's not allowed to possess or own any firearms, because of this order. (nervously) That's what it says.
Thank you. Do you know whether your ex-husband was ever formally served with this order, or that he otherwise knew about it and its terms?

(nodding) Yes, I'm sure he was served with the order. I remember my lawyer telling me that he was personally served with the papers at his residence, 1456 Oakwood Drive. And... (thinking) he's mentioned it to me before, so I know he's aware of the terms. He's never really respected the order, though... (trailing off, looking concerned)

You mentioned he called you earlier today and said 'you'll be sorry.' Can you tell me approximately what time that call came in, what number he called from, and whether there was anything else said during the call beyond that phrase?

(thinking) The call came in around 9:45 PM. I'm not sure what number he called from, but I think it was his cell phone. (pausing) As for what was said... he just said 'you'll be sorry' and then hung up. It was really short, but it scared me because of the way he said it. (nervously) That's all that was said, just those three words.
Where were you when you received that call — were you at your home at 14572 Wilder Boulevard? And do you have a record of that call in your phone — for example, in your recent calls or a voicemail?

(nodding) Yes, I was at home when I received the call. I live in the upstairs apartment at 14572 Wilder Boulevard. (checking her phone) And yes, I have a record of the call in my recent calls. It's listed as a call from his number, and it shows the time it came in. (looking at her phone) I also have a recording of the call, actually. I was able to record it because I was so scared and I wanted to have proof of what he said. (looking up) Would you like to hear it?

Yes, please — that recording is important evidence. Please do not delete it. I want to make sure that recording is preserved. The call log, his number, and the recording are all potentially useful. Separate from the phone call — is your ex-husband at or near your residence right now, or has he been there physically today?

(shaking her head) No, he's not here right now. I didn't see him or hear from him in person today, just the phone call. (thinking) And I don't think he's been here physically today, no. I've been here most of the day, and I would have noticed if he came by. (looking around nervously) My roommate, Emily, is here too, and she would have told me if she saw him. (pausing) But like I said, he did call me and say those scary things, and that's what really upset me.
You mentioned your roommate Emily was home. Did Emily hear the call or know about it — for example, did you tell her about it right away or did she hear any part of the conversation?

(nodding) Yes, Emily was here when I got the call. She didn't hear the actual conversation, but I told her about it right after it happened. I was really upset and scared, and I needed to talk to someone. (thinking) She's actually the one who encouraged me to call the police and report it. She's been a really good support for me throughout all of this. (pausing) If you need to talk to her, she's here and she's willing to give a statement.

Thank you — I will want to speak with Emily as well. Do you know Emily's last name? Also, has your ex-husband contacted you in any other way today — texts, emails, social media, anyone reaching out on his behalf — or was this phone call the only contact?

(thinking) Emily's last name is Patel. And as for other contact... no, the phone call was the only direct contact I've had with him today. (pausing) I did check my social media and email, and I didn't see any messages from him or anyone else that seemed suspicious or related to him. (shaking her head) Just the phone call. It was really out of the blue, and it scared me.
Has your ex-husband violated this protection order before today, or made unwanted contact with you on prior occasions that you reported or did not report?

(hesitantly) Yes... he's violated the order before. There have been several incidents over the past two years where he's contacted me or shown up at my workplace or home. (pausing, looking uncomfortable) I've reported some of them, but not all of them. I've tried to just avoid him and move on with my life, but it's hard when he keeps doing things like this. (sighing) The most recent incident before today was on January 25, 2023. That's when he showed up at my workplace and caused a scene. (shaking her head) It's just really frustrating and scary.
Are you physically safe right now? Were there any injuries, property damage, or medical concerns as a result of what happened today?

(shaking her head) No, I'm not hurt or anything. I'm just really scared and shaken up. (pausing) There wasn't any property damage or anything like that. He just made that threatening phone call, and it really upset me. (taking a deep breath) I'm safe right now, I think. I'm just worried about what might happen next. (looking around nervously) That's why I'm glad you're here. I feel a little better with you here.

I want to make sure you have all the support you need. Would you like information about victim services or advocacy organizations that can help you? And before we finish, I want to give you one more opportunity to provide your ex-husband's name for the report — even a first name or a nickname would help. If not, that is okay. Is there anything else about the order, today's incident, or any other contacts you think I should include?

(nodding) Yes, please, I would like that information. I could use all the help I can get right now. (pausing, thinking) And... (taking a deep breath) his name is... (hesitating, then whispering) Michael. My ex-husband's name is Michael. (looking up, tears in her eyes) I'm sorry it took me so long to say it. (composing herself) As for anything else... I just want to make sure he can't hurt me or anyone else. I'm scared of what he might do next. (pausing) That's all, I think. Just please, make sure he can't hurt me.

Thank you, Kaitlyn. I know that was not easy to say. We will do everything we can to follow up on this. Do you know Michael's last name, or any other identifying details about him — such as his date of birth, address, or vehicle — that would help us locate him if needed?

(nodding) Yes, his last name is... (pausing, then saying) Reyes. Michael Reyes. (thinking) He's my ex-husband, so we have the same last name. (providing more information) I think he's 35 years old, and his date of birth is... (pausing, trying to remember) November 12, 1987. (thinking) He lives at 1456 Oakwood Drive, and he drives a... (describing the vehicle) a black truck, a Ford F-150, I think. (providing the license plate number) The license plate number is... (pausing, trying to remember) 6GWN421. (looking up) I hope that helps.

narrative:
NARRATIVE REPORT — PROTECTION ORDER VIOLATION

DISPATCH / ARRIVAL

I responded to a call for service at 14572 Wilder Boulevard regarding an alleged violation of a domestic violence restraining order. Dispatch advised that the caller, identified as Kaitlyn Elara Reyes, reported that her ex-husband had contacted her despite an active protection order. Upon arrival, I made contact with Ms. Reyes at the upstairs apartment at that address.

PARTIES

Caller / Protected Party: Kaitlyn Elara Reyes, residing at 14572 Wilder Boulevard (upstairs apartment).

Restrained Party: Michael Reyes, ex-husband of Kaitlyn Elara Reyes. Ms. Reyes identified him as approximately 35 years of age with a date of birth of November 12, 1987. She stated he resides at 1456 Oakwood Drive. She described his vehicle as a black Ford F-150, license plate 6GWN421.

Witness: Emily Patel, roommate of Ms. Reyes, also present at the residence.

ORDER DETAILS

Ms. Reyes produced a physical copy of what she described as a domestic violence restraining order bearing case number DV-2022-001. According to Ms. Reyes, the order was issued on February 10, 2022, and does not carry an expiration date. She stated the order reads that it is "in effect until further order of the court."

Ms. Reyes read the following terms from the order: (1) Michael Reyes is prohibited from contacting her, either directly or indirectly; (2) he is prohibited from calling, texting, or appearing at her home or workplace; (3) he is required to remain at least 100 yards away from her at all times; and (4) he is prohibited from possessing or owning any firearms.

Regarding service of the order, Ms. Reyes stated that her attorney informed her that Michael Reyes was personally served with the order at his residence at 1456 Oakwood Drive. Ms. Reyes also stated that Michael Reyes had referenced the order to her on prior occasions, indicating his awareness of its terms.

VIOLATION

Ms. Reyes reported that at approximately 9:45 PM on the date of this incident, she received a phone call at her home at 14572 Wilder Boulevard. She stated she believed the call came from Michael Reyes's cell phone, though she was not certain of the specific number. During the call, she stated Michael Reyes said, "you'll be sorry," and then immediately ended the call. Ms. Reyes described the call as very brief, consisting only of that phrase. She stated that the tone in which the words were spoken frightened her.

Ms. Reyes stated this telephone contact constituted a direct violation of the order's no-contact and prohibition-on-calling terms. Michael Reyes was not physically present at the residence at the time of this report or at any point during the day; the violation was limited to the phone call described above.

Ms. Reyes confirmed that she checked her social media and email following the call and found no additional messages from Michael Reyes or from anyone acting on his behalf. The phone call was the only contact that occurred on this date.

STATEMENTS

Ms. Reyes stated (in substance): "My ex-husband called me earlier today and he made some really scary comments. He said 'you'll be sorry' during the call and it really frightened me." She further stated: "He just said 'you'll be sorry' and then hung up. It was really short, but it scared me because of the way he said it."

Regarding the order, Ms. Reyes stated: "It says here that he's not allowed to contact me, either directly or indirectly. He's not supposed to call me, text me, or show up at my home or workplace. It also says he has to stay at least 100 yards away from me at all times. And it says he's not allowed to possess or own any firearms."

Regarding her ex-husband's knowledge of the order, Ms. Reyes stated: "He's mentioned it to me before, so I know he's aware of the terms. He's never really respected the order."

Witness Emily Patel was present at the residence at the time the call was received. Ms. Patel did not directly hear the conversation, but Ms. Reyes stated she informed Ms. Patel of the call immediately afterward. Ms. Patel encouraged Ms. Reyes to contact police and indicated she was willing to provide a statement to officers.

EVIDENCE

The following evidence was identified during the interview:

1. Physical copy of the domestic violence restraining order (case number DV-2022-001, issued February 10, 2022), in Ms. Reyes's possession at the scene.

2. Call log on Ms. Reyes's cell phone, showing an incoming call attributed to Michael Reyes's number at approximately 9:45 PM on the date of this incident.

3. Audio recording of the phone call, made by Ms. Reyes on her cell phone at the time of the call. Ms. Reyes stated she recorded it because she was frightened and wanted documentation of what was said. Ms. Reyes was advised to preserve this recording and not to delete it.

PRIOR VIOLATIONS / HISTORY

Ms. Reyes stated that Michael Reyes has violated the protection order on multiple occasions over the approximately two-year period since the order was issued. She stated some of those incidents were reported to law enforcement and some were not. She identified the most recent prior incident as occurring on January 25, 2023, when Michael Reyes appeared at her workplace and "caused a scene."

SAFETY / RISK

At the time of this report, Michael Reyes was not present at or near 14572 Wilder Boulevard. Ms. Reyes reported no physical injuries, no property damage, and no medical needs as a result of the incident. She stated she was physically safe but expressed fear about potential future conduct by Michael Reyes. No weapons were mentioned, displayed, or accessed during the incident.

DISPOSITION / FOLLOW-UP

Ms. Reyes was advised to preserve all electronic evidence, including the phone call recording and the call log on her device. Ms. Reyes requested and was provided information about victim services and advocacy organizations. The physical copy of the protection order was reviewed at the scene. Emily Patel was noted as an available witness willing to give a statement.

Follow-up is needed to: (1) verify the current status of case number DV-2022-001 through court records; (2) attempt to locate and contact Michael Reyes regarding this incident; (3) obtain and preserve the audio recording from Ms. Reyes's device; and (4) review prior reported violations associated with this order.
